items.title: Woman 'still here to see grandkids grow' welcomes free HIV treatment in SA

children.content: When Kath Leane was diagnosed with HIV almost 40 years ago, she feared she would never see her children — let alone her grandchildren — grow up. But the antiretroviral treatment which helped make that possible is today being made free for all South Australians.

gallery.altText: A grandmother with long blond hair sits in an armchair in her home with a bookcase behind her

thumbnail.altText: A grandmother with long blond hair sits in an armchair in her home with a bookcase behind her

items.title: 'One last crack' to restart Whyalla’s blast furnace as workers face nervous wait

children.content: The aging blast furnace at the Whyalla steelworks could be permanently shut down before the steelworks transitions to a direct-reduced iron plant and electric arc furnace, placing hundreds of jobs at risk.

image.altText: A man wearing an orange jacket and white hard hat stands outside a large industrial building

items.title: Thunderbirds co-captain to lead 'connected' team in grand final repeat

children.content: The Adelaide Thunderbirds are looking to win their third premiership in four years, with co-captain Georgie Horjus putting their success down to strong team connections on and off the court.

image.altText: Three women playing netball in a stadium

items.title: 'No genuine opportunity' to prevent crash that killed two women, coroner finds

children.content: A South Australian coroner says it is an "unfortunate truth" that there was no way for police to predict or prevent a collision that killed a senior police officer and another woman in 2020.

gallery.altText: An image of Joanne Shanahan and Tania McNeill.

items.title: Four fatal crashes in 12 days rock SA's Limestone Coast

children.content: Police are calling on drivers to stay attentive and slow down after four people were killed on one region's roads in just 12 days.

image.altText: A bunch of flowers placed next to a road.

items.title: Midwife hit with $70,000 foreign investment tax after moving to Australia

children.content: A British midwife who migrated to Australia to work at a regional hospital during statewide staff shortages is calling for changes to the law after she was slugged $70,000 by state and federal authorities for the purchase of her home.

image.altText: A British midwife with brown hair stands next to her husband in front of their home in Whyalla

items.title: Temporary ban to protect Whyalla's 'significantly low' cuttlefish numbers

children.content: From Wednesday, a 90-day exclusion will begin at the cuttlefish sanctuary zone between Black Point and Stony Point in South Australia's Upper Spencer Gulf.

image.altText: two large cuttlefish near rocks underwater

items.title: Rex Airlines breached continuous disclosure obligations

children.content: Rex contravened its continuous disclosure obligations, misleading the market, the NSW Supreme Court found on Tuesday.

image.altText: a plane flies in the sky

items.title: Tourism operator says 'silence is deafening' about how to spot bird flu cases

children.content: A tourism operator on South Australia's coast is frustrated by what he says is poor communication from authorities about bird flu. He says he saw sick birds weeks ago but did not know to report the sighting.

image.altText: A giant petrel bird hides in green seaside foliage at Knights Beach on the Fleurieu Peninsula.

items.title: Randall says concussion left her 'no choice' but to retire from AFLW

children.content: Adelaide Crows star Chelsea Randall announces her retirement from the AFLW, citing concussion as a major factor in her decision to hang up her boots and step away from the game.

image.altText: Adelaide Crows AFLW star Chelsea Randall.

items.title: Thousands of outback roads damaged by floods could take year to fix

children.content: Damage to South Australia's network of outback roads is isolating remote communities, preventing children from getting to school, and slowing the restocking process for pastoralists recovering from drought.

image.altText: A man holding a tape measure down the edge of a washed out dirt road

items.title: Network 10 strikes last-minute deal to extend broadcasting in SA and NSW

children.content: Two regions in South Australia and one in New South Wales were set to lose access to free-to-air Network 10 services in coming days, but a new agreement means coverage will continue until the end of September.

image.altText: Sign of the number 10 in a blue circle on a tower with trees visible

items.title: Home prices 'fully in decline' with biggest national fall since 2022

children.content: Australian home values record their biggest decline in more than three years as the housing market slowdown that began in Sydney and Melbourne spreads across the country.

items.title: Family who spent $250,000 on IVF welcomes new rebate scheme in SA

children.content: A South Australian couple who spent $250,000 on fertility treatments say the government's IVF rebate will make a "huge difference" for people trying to conceive.

image.altText: A man holds a toddler while a woman speaks into media microphones outside a house

items.title: Accounting firms could be split in wake of KPMG audit scandal

children.content: Accounting firms could be asked to split their lucrative consulting services from their audit functions and individual firm partners could face far greater scrutiny and penalties for legal breaches, under a shake up of the industry being consulted on by the federal government.

items.title: How to encourage your child to play sport and protect their head from knocks

children.content: When your child decides they are keen on playing sport, there are lots of factors to consider as a parent, and after recent news stories about chronic traumatic encephalopathy, keeping them safe from head injuries may be front of mind.

items.title: Renewed push to ban 'conversion practices' in Tasmania

children.content: Tasmania was the last Australian jurisdiction to decriminalise homosexuality, but since then it has gone on to pass some of the country's most inclusive legislation. There's one final frontier — conversion practices.

items.title: The condition that causes people to get lost in their own home

children.content: Some estimates suggest up to one in 30 people may be affected by a condition that has been described as a lifelong inability to orient oneself — even in extremely familiar surroundings.

items.title: Intelligence reports Germany under threat from extremists and foreign powers

children.content: Moscow-recruited saboteurs, Chinese spies, Iran-backed Islamist militants and violent neo-Nazis are growing problems, says intelligence service BfV.

items.title: Manhunt in Monaco after parcel bomb injures Ukrainian businessman

children.content: Police have declined to identify the presumed target of the blast, but several sources say it was Ukrainian-born businessman Vadym Yermolaiev, a permanent resident of Monaco who has acquired Cypriot nationality

items.title: Usman Khawaja helps launch national Islamophobia awareness campaign

children.content: The retired 39-year-old, who was the first Muslim to play international cricket for Australia, says Islamophobia is on the rise, with women most vulnerable to attacks, because "hate is more prevalent that it has been before".

items.title: This Liberal took his teacher to gambling anonymous

children.content: When Liberal backbencher Simon Kennedy was a teenager, one of his high school teachers asked to borrow money for his gambling addiction. He says he ended up accompanying the teacher to Gamblers Anonymous meetings.
